message:
fix(mcp_tools): reject timeout_secs=0 in ca.{search,view,status} (ft-tzwuw)

The three WaCass* MCP tools declare a JSON-Schema constraint of
`"timeout_secs": { "minimum": 1, "default": 15 }`, but serde_json does
not honour JSON-Schema numeric bounds on its own. A client sending
`timeout_secs: 0` therefore reached cass_client_with_timeout(0) →
Duration::from_secs(0) → `timeout_with_cx` fired before the child
`cass` binary ever had a chance to execute, returning a confusing
"cass timeout (0 secs)" error on every call. The user-visible shape was
"my ca.search always times out"; the actual cause was a zero sentinel
passed down three layers before being noticed.

Same failure mode as ft-t62hq (which fixed the equivalent bug on
wa.wait_for / wa.send). This commit applies the same pre-dispatch guard
to the three cass-side tools that share the `timeout_secs` schema:

- WaCassSearchTool::call    (ca.search)
- WaCassViewTool::call      (ca.view)
- WaCassStatusTool::call    (ca.status)

Each guard is the minimum viable check — a single `if == 0` branch
returning MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS with a message that quotes the schema
("timeout_secs must be >= 1 (got 0)") and a hint telling the caller to
omit the field to get the default of 15. The guard runs before any
runtime construction, so zero-timeout calls now fail fast and cheap
instead of spinning up tokio just to trip an instant deadline.

Pinning tests added for all three surfaces:

- ft_tzwuw_ca_search_rejects_zero_timeout_secs
- ft_tzwuw_ca_view_rejects_zero_timeout_secs
- ft_tzwuw_ca_status_rejects_zero_timeout_secs

Each asserts `ok=false`, `error_code=MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS`, and the
exact error substring, so a future regression that re-introduces the
zero-timeout dispatch path fails loudly at the MCP surface rather than
at the cass binary.

No behaviour change for any non-zero value; the default (15) path is
unchanged.
